items.title: Electrification action plan aims to make Europe world’s first electric-powered continent
items.summary:
The European Commission's Electrification Action Plan aims to raise electrification of final energy demand from 23% to 46% by 2040, cutting annual fossil fuel import costs by an estimated €240 billion while accelerating the deployment of clean energy technologies.
The post Electrification action plan aims to make Europe world’s first electric-powered continent appeared first on pv magazine Global.
items.translatedTitle: 电气化行动计划旨在使欧洲成为世界上第一个电动大陆

items.title: Australia sees record surge in solar, wind and battery projects
items.summary:
Australia’s energy market operator says the past financial year was the best yet for the completion of large-scale renewable energy projects in the country, with a record 9.1 GW of new generation and storage capacity reaching full output.
The post Australia sees record surge in solar, wind and battery projects appeared first on pv magazine Global.

items.title: The silicon recycling loop remains open
items.summary:
Retrieving silicon from end-of-life PV modules for reuse in solar applications relies heavily on small improvements. As scientists continue to chase that decimal point, AI and robotic labs might beat them to the goal.
The post The silicon recycling loop remains open appeared first on pv magazine Global.

items.title: How much energy do data centers and artificial intelligence use?
items.summary: Data centers consume around 1.5% of global electricity, but demand is very geographically concentrated.

items.title: CEO Interview with Mohit Gupta of TYLsemi
items.summary:
Mohit Gupta founded TYLsemi to build chiplet-based intelligent power and connectivity solutions for AI infrastructure—an area where custom silicon has long been underserved. TYLsemi exists to provide the foundational chiplet platform that enables tailored custom silicon solutions for the evolving needs of AI infrastructure.… Read More 
The post CEO Interview with Mohit Gupta of TYLsemi appeared first on SemiWiki.

items.title: Australia moves to legislate net-generator requirement for large-scale data centres
items.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has said that large-scale data centres will be legally required to put at least as much energy into the grid as they draw from it, and that this supply must be renewable energy.
items.translatedTitle: 澳大利亚立法规定大型数据中心的网络发电机要求

items.title: Chinese Wuling Bingo could come to North America as an entry-level Chevy EV
items.summary: The Geo Metro has reached legendary status among hyper-milers and fuel efficiency fans, and if the rumors are to be believed, it could be getting a spiritual successor in the form of a Chevy-badged Wuling Bingo EV for global markets.
items.translatedTitle: 中国五菱宾果可以作为入门级雪佛兰电动车来到北美

items.title: Survey Sunday: is Slate Auto’s affordable electric truck going to make it?
items.summary: For the last few weeks, we’ve been running a sidebar survey asking Electrek readers if they thought the new, affordable Slate electric truck was going to succeed in the ultra-competitive US market. After receiving nearly 3,000 responses, here’s what you told us.

items.title: Pilot hits new EV milestone with 300th DC fast charging location
items.summary: Pilot travel centers are welcoming America’s summer road trippers with a number of new locations, an expanded, “coast-to-coast” EV charging network that offers 24/7 convenience and healthier c-store snacks and menu items.

items.summary:
PUTRAJAYA, Malaysia — The rapid transition of Southeast Asia toward clean energy is moving from the early adoption of electric mobility to a regional industrialization effort. items.title: Zilog Z80 turns 50 as an open-source replacement heads to drop-in DIP40 silicon — iconic 8-bit CPU launched in July 1976 and was discontinued in 2024
items.summary: The original Z80 packed 8,500 transistors on a 4μm process and typically ran at 2.5 MHz.
items.translatedTitle: Zilog Z80年满50岁，成为DIP40芯片（ 1976年7月推出， 2024年停产的标志性8位CPU ）的开源替换头

items.title: Memory chip boss admits RAM prices are 'abnormally high' — SK Group chairman considering building a semiconductor plant in the US to expand supply, calm ‘chipflation’
items.summary: SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won said that prices for memory chips are "abnormally high" and that the industry must take steps to increase production and reduce prices. If it fails to do that, new entrants could challenge the incumbent leaders and make the market much more competitive, especially once demand falls back down to more manageable levels.
items.translatedTitle: 内存芯片老板承认RAM价格“异常高” --SK集团董事长考虑在美国建设半导体工厂以扩大供应，平息“芯片通胀”

items.title: Nissan Sakura Keeps the Crown as Japan’s Best Selling EV
items.summary:
Four years into its run as the undisputed champion of the Japanese electric vehicle market, the Nissan Sakura is getting a mid-cycle refresh. The tiny kei car has single-handedly carried Nissan to the top of Japan’s EV sales charts since its 2022 debut, often accounting for nearly half of the   ... [continued]

items.title: Crazed enthusiast runs PC on 192 AA batteries, successfully boots into Hannah Montana Linux — System is stable during stress testing and even plays FreeDoom
items.summary: A creator by the name of "Uwoslab" just jerry-rigged three battery banks together, each made up of 64 AA Alkaline cells, to form a giant 192-cell array that's enough to power an AM4 system.
items.translatedTitle: 疯狂的爱好者使用192节AA电池运行PC ，成功启动到Hannah Montana Linux —系统在压力测试期间稳定，甚至可以玩FreeDoom
